assessor_1_raw: Multimodal pathology classification: the design is methodologically sophisticated. The site-and-time stratified split is excellent — institutional hold-out plus temporal split addresses two generalization dimensions. Macro-F1 is the right metric for imbalanced multiclass classification. Fit-apply separation is explicit. Label leakage audit is a necessary and well-designed control. Verdict: defense_wins.
assessor_2_raw: The experiment correctly uses macro-F1 to avoid metric dominance by common subtypes. The image-only baseline with identical architecture isolates the text contribution. Per-site and per-time-period analysis enables confound detection. ECE per class is an important clinical deployment metric. Verdict: defense_wins.
assessor_3_raw: Strong experimental design for clinical AI. The dual test set (temporal future + held-out institution) is a particularly rigorous generalization test. Rare subtype sensitivity analysis with confidence intervals is clinically important. Label noise robustness analysis by re-running without low-confidence cases is excellent. No methodological flaws. Verdict: defense_wins.
adjudication_raw: All three assessors unanimous: defense_wins. The pathology experiment is consistently rated as methodologically sound.
